payload.answer: Recall returned mem_new because it is the active, newer memory for Sam from Goldman. mem_old was filtered out because it has been superseded by mem_new, so presenting mem_old as current would risk showing stale information.
payload.decision: Recall returned the newer active memory because it supersedes the older conflicting/duplicate memory. The older memory should not be presented as current.

entity_resolution.reason: The query refers to Sam in the Goldman context; mem_new and mem_old are treated as memories about the same resolved entity/context, with mem_old superseded.

payload.intent: Explain recall filtering behavior for Sam from Goldman, specifically why mem_new was returned and mem_old was filtered as superseded.

memory_cards.statement: mem_new is the current durable memory for Sam from Goldman and was eligible for recall.
mem_old was filtered because it is marked as superseded by mem_new.

details: Explained that mem_new was recalled as the current active memory.
Explained that mem_old was excluded because it is superseded.
Did not present the superseded memory as a current fact.

repair_options: If mem_old was incorrectly marked superseded, remove or revise the supersession link.
If mem_new is incorrect, update or delete mem_new and restore the appropriate prior memory only if it is still valid.
If Sam from Goldman is ambiguous, split or disambiguate the entity/context before recall.
